item.title: Pakistan Inflation Cools, Keeping Central Bank on Guard
item.description: Pakistan’s inflation eased last month, though it remained in double digits, keeping policymakers on guard against persistent price pressures.

item.title: Euro-Zone Inflation Slows More Than Expected as Oil Retreats
item.description: Euro-area inflation eased more than anticipated as efforts toward peace in the Middle East sent global oil prices lower.

item.title: ByteDance Picks Brazil for Its Largest Data Center Outside China
item.description: The TikTok creator’s $39 billion data center is the latest payoff from Lula’s courtship of Chinese investment.

item.title: The Wall Street Women Who Traded Finance Careers for Influencer Success
item.description: These pros ditched spreadsheets and trading floors to go after brand deals and social media fame.

item.title: Gilt Market Is Giving Burnham a Pass For Now, Says Aegon’s Lynch
item.description: Gilt markets have shrugged off the lack of economic policy clarity from Andy Burnham, the likely successor to replace Keir Starmer as UK prime minister, according to Aegon Asset Management’s James Lynch.

item.title: ECB’s Kocher Says the Next Decisions Will Be Either Hike or Hold
item.description: The European Central Bank’s next policy decisions will come down to either hiking interest rates further or holding them steady, Governing Council member Martin Kocher said.

item.title: Germany’s Pension Fund Would Be Safe With Bundesbank, Nagel Says
item.description: Germany’s central bank is well positioned to oversee the country’s planned state-backed pension fund if it’s asked to do so, according to President Joachim Nagel.

item.title: Alphabet’s $2 Trillion Gain Turns ‘Rock Star’ Into Question Mark
item.description: Alphabet Inc. has had a remarkable 12 months by just about any measure you can imagine.

item.title: ECB’s Nagel Says He’s Keeping Options Open for Next Two Meetings
item.description: European Central Bank Governing Council member Joachim Nagel said he’s keeping an open mind on what action is needed at the next two interest-rate meetings, with uncertainly remaining over the situation in the Middle East and its effect on inflation.

item.title: Galderma Slumps After FDA Turns Down Botox Rival Relfydess
item.description: Galderma Group AG shares slumped the most in more than a year after the US Food and Drug Administration turned down the Swiss dermatology firm’s rival Botox treatment Relfydess.

item.title: Eni, Mercuria to Form Global Energy Commodities Trading Venture
item.description: Italian oil company Eni SpA and commodities merchant Mercuria Energy Group Ltd. signed a deal to trade energy together globally.

item.title: TikTok Eyes About 300 More Job Cuts at European Hub in Dublin
item.description: TikTok is considering cutting about 300 jobs at its European hub in Dublin, following a similarly sized round of layoffs last year.

item.title: South32’s New CEO Looks to Focus on Copper After Aluminum Deal
item.description: South32 Ltd. will focus on copper and zinc after selling off the aluminum business that accounted for 60% of earnings, with the disposal announced on Chief Executive Officer Matt Daley’s first day in the role.

item.title: French Gas Users to See Higher Bills on Network Costs, CRE Says
item.description: French natural gas consumers are set to pay more for network maintenance over the coming decades as falling demand for the fuel means the infrastructure will be financed by fewer users, the industry regulator said.

item.title: Primark Sales Fall as AB Foods Prepares to Split Off Chain
item.description: Associated British Foods Plc reported lower sales at its Primark budget fashion chain and a profit-hit at its sugar unit due to the war in Iran, as the conglomerate prepares to separate its clothing and food divisions.

item.title: UK House Prices Stagnate as Cheaper Loans Bring Limited Relief
item.description: UK house prices failed to grow for a second straight month in June, Nationwide Building Society said, as mortgage rates held above levels seen before the Iran war despite recently easing.

item.title: Europe’s Bank Bulls See More Upside After 21% Quarterly Gain
item.description: Following a stellar second-quarter rally for European banks, investors expect one of the region’s hottest equity trades to keep delivering.

item.title: Abu Dhabi’s MGX Raises $49 Billion for One of the Biggest Ever AI Funds
item.description: MGX has raised $49 billion for one of the biggest ever funds dedicated to artificial intelligence deals, propelling the two-year-old Abu Dhabi firm into the ranks of the most consequential investors in the sector globally.

item.title: Bonds Fall Ahead of Sintra Event With Central Bankers
Kim Dotcom Loses Latest Appeal in Case Against Extradition to US
item.description: Kim Dotcom, the internet entrepreneur facing extradition from New Zealand, has lost the latest round in his legal battle to avoid facing prosecution in the US.

item.title: India’s $2 Billion State Stake Sales Buck Equity Market Slowdown
item.description: India’s federal government raised $2 billion through stake sales in listed state-run companies last quarter, providing a rare bright spot for the country’s subdued capital market after the conflict in the Middle East slowed broader equity issuance.

item.title: Thailand Gets Serious About Chasing High-Spending Tourists
item.description: Thailand is done chasing tourist records. After decades of measuring success by ever-rising arrival numbers, the government is making its clearest push yet to prioritize how much visitors spend versus how many show up.

item.title: South Africa Plans to Tap Existing Rand Sukuk Bonds This Year
item.description: South Africa will tap existing rand-denominated sukuk bonds as part of its funding plans for the current fiscal year.

item.title: Japan’s FX Chief Says Past Yen Intervention Has Shown Impact
item.description: Foreign exchange intervention by Japan to support the yen two months ago was successful, and some US authorities voiced support, the nation’s top currency official said, as the yen hovers at a four-decade low.

item.title: OCBC to Raise Annual Tech Spending to More Than $771 Million
item.description: Oversea-Chinese Banking Corp. plans to increase its annual technology spending, including on artificial intelligence, to more than S$1 billion ($771 million), said a top executive.

item.title: An English Furniture Maker Faces AI Era of Bots Buying Sofas
item.description: With origins in the English countryside, The Cotswold Company is known for upscale furniture that evokes its bucolic backstory. Now it’s bracing for the era of artificial intelligence.

item.title: RBI’s Short Dollar Book Hit Record in May Before Policy Measures
item.description: The Reserve Bank of India’s short dollar book rose to a record $106.7 billion in May as the central bank used derivatives to defend the currency.

item.title: A Giant Cable Exposes Ireland’s AI Ambitions and Security Risks
item.description: A new Amazon transatlantic fiber-optic link is symbolic of the country’s tech economy, but also of its chronic lack of defense spending.

item.title: In Hindsight, Will a Coming Crash Seem Obvious?
item.description: The template for 2008 holds up after a midyear check on our imaginary hedge fund.

item.title: Global M&A Tops $2.5 Trillion After First-Half Deals Surge
item.description: The year was tipped to be a potential blockbuster in deals and the first half delivered, setting a pace that is likely to continue in the closing months of 2026.
